overview.minides: Nissan's Patrol-inspired mid-size SUV — a platform sibling of the Renault Duster — with C-shaped LED lights, 518L boot, 10.25-inch digital cluster, Level 2 ADAS, and two turbo-petrol engines.
overview.description: Launched on 9 July 2026, the Nissan Tekton marks the brand's return to the fiercely competitive mid-size SUV segment. Priced from ₹10.49 lakh to ₹18.59 lakh, it shares its CMF-CD platform with the Renault Duster but sports a completely distinct Patrol-inspired design — chrome V-Motion grille, C-shaped LED headlamps, connected C-shaped LED tail lamps, and flush roof rails. Available in 6 variants (Visia, Visia+, Acenta, N-Connecta, Tekna, Tekna+) across 2 engines: a 1.0L 3-cylinder turbo (100 hp, MT only) and a 1.3L 4-cylinder turbo (160 hp, MT or 6-speed DCT). The Tekton scores 5 stars in Bharat NCAP. Key highlights include a 10.1-inch Tek-Link infotainment with Google Built-in, 10.25-inch digital cluster, panoramic sunroof, 6-speaker Arkamys audio, 6-way powered + ventilated front seats, 48-colour ambient lighting, dual-zone climate control, 360-degree camera, and Level 2 ADAS on Tekna and Tekna+ trims. Bookings open from launch day at ₹11,000; deliveries begin 20 July 2026.

sharedSpecs.platform: Renault-Nissan CMF-CD (Common Module Family — C/D segment)

variants.name: Tekton Acenta MT
keyFeatures: Auto Climate Control + Rear AC Vents
